components.description: All components used in this converter. Each entry is a thin envelope around an PEAS document, carrying the in-design context (refdes name, role, optional pins/notes) that does not belong inside the PEAS doc itself.

circuit.description: All connections between components: wires (electrical nets shared by 2+ pins), couplings (magnetic coupling between windings), and externalPorts (terminals of the converter to the outside world). Discriminated by the `kind` field. Parasitic L/C/R are NOT a kind — they are modelled as real components in the components array, so they appear in the BOM.

simulation.description: Optional SPICE-specific overrides: model definitions, simulation commands, per-component parameter overrides. Pure topology documents (not yet simulated) omit this entirely.

name.description: Reference designator unique within this converter (e.g. 'Q1', 'D1', 'L_pri', 'C_in', 'R_sense'). Connection endpoints refer to components by this name.

role.description: What this component does in this specific converter. Contextual to the design — the same MOSFET part number could be a highSideSwitch in one converter and a lowSideSwitch in another, so role cannot live inside the PEAS document. Notes: PSFB/totem-pole bridge switches use primarySwitch/secondarySwitch (primary H-bridge), highSideSwitch/lowSideSwitch (secondary or fast-leg), or synchronousRectifier (slow-leg diode-mode FETs) with usageNotes for clarification. ACF auxiliary switch = auxiliarySwitch. Controller ICs (LLC, QR, CrM controllers) = controller.

data.description: The component itself. Either a full PEAS document inline, or a URI string into a TAS data file. Always identify by partNumber when using URIs (e.g. 'TAS/data/mosfets.ndjson?partNumber=C3M0032120K'); never by line number, since NDJSON files may be reordered.

name.description: Connection name. For wires this is the node name (e.g. 'sw_node', 'Vbus', 'gnd'). For couplings, a unique magnetic-coupling identifier (e.g. 'K_main'). For externalPorts, the terminal name (e.g. 'Vin+', 'Vout-', 'EN').

kind.description: wire = purely internal electrical net; 2+ pins share a node. coupling = magnetic coupling between inductor windings (no shared node — translates to a SPICE K-element). externalPort = an electrical net that ALSO terminates at an external converter terminal (Vin/Vout/GND/EN/FB). An externalPort is a wire — it carries the same 2+ internal pins that a `wire` would, plus the connection name doubles as the external terminal label. Do NOT create both a `wire` and an `externalPort` for the same net; pick one.

endpoints.description: List of (component, pin) endpoints connected by this entry. A wire on a 3-pin SW node has 3 endpoints. A coupling between two windings has 2 endpoints with no pin field (the entire winding is coupled). Order is not significant.

component.description: Refers to a component.name in topology.components.

pin.description: Pin on the referenced component. Required for kind = wire / externalPort. Omit for kind = coupling, where the connection is to the whole winding.

parameters.description: Connection-specific extras. The set of meaningful keys depends on the connection kind.

coupling.description: Magnetic coupling coefficient k for kind=coupling. 1.0 = ideal, 0.999 = tightly coupled transformer, 0.5 = loose coupling. REQUIRED when kind=coupling.

direction.description: For kind=externalPort: whether the converter sources, sinks, or both at this terminal.

allOf.$comment: Couplings must specify their k factor.
Wire and externalPort endpoints must name a pin. Coupling endpoints may omit it (whole-winding link).

simulation.description: SPICE-specific data needed to run a simulation against this topology. Optional — converters that have not been simulated yet omit this block. Keeping it under topology (rather than at TAS root) makes it clear that the simulation describes THIS topology, not a sibling concept.

models.description: SPICE .model and .subckt definitions referenced by elementOverrides[].model.

name.description: Model/subcircuit name as referenced in elementOverrides.

text.description: Raw SPICE text of the .model or .subckt definition.

elementOverrides.description: Per-component SPICE overrides keyed by component.name. Only present for components whose simulation behaviour cannot be inferred from the PEAS document alone. NOTE: there is no `value` override here on purpose — component values come from the PEAS doc and are the single source of truth. Only model and parameters overrides are allowed.

model.description: Name of a model defined in simulation.models[] (or an externally available .lib name).

parameters.description: Extra SPICE parameters (initial conditions, temperature coefficients, etc.) e.g. {"ic": 0, "tc1": 0.003}.
